a:r.a:t: Topic
Status
HGV Observations
Initial Recommendations
Eligibility
All marketing roles with comp plans appear to meet the three eligibility criteria.
N/A – All roles on plans meet standard eligibility criteria
Target Cash Compensation
Frontline and manager roles are well aligned with direct competitors and typically fall at or above the 50
th
percentile of the vacation ownership industry
Directors and above generally align with industry competitors but lag compared to the broader market, potentially limiting cross-industry talent attraction
Consider compensation levels for directors and above, where we understand recruiting has been difficult
Aligning more to the broader industry to may assist in hiring talent externally
Pay Mix & Leverage
HGV pay mix is more variable-heavy than its direct competitors and vacation ownership industry data, with peers typically around 50/50 to 60/40 (base/variable) vs HGV’s 40/60
Director+ pay mixes are similar to frontline; typically, pay mixes are more base-heavy in management positions allowing more focus on leadership/strategy.
Consider shifting pay mix towards base compensation to better align to market expectations
Measures & Weights
Marketing roles tend to have 3-4 measures in their compensation plans.
For some roles, there are measures are weighted to less than 20% of variable pay.
Many plans use a high number of performance tiers (8+ in many cases, with 3 being target level)
The selected measures appear to be outcome oriented and are appropriate for their role.
Where there are 4+ measures or measures with <20% weighting, consider consolidating or removing measures with duplicative purposes
Simplify payout curves and reduce the number of tiers
Compensation Ranges
Currently unable to assess with the data received
Unable to fully assess compensation ranges with the data provided at this time
Payment Periods
Marketing comp plan periods are mostly set to monthly cycles, aligning to the high volume and short sales cycle focus of the industry
Commissions payouts are made on a biweekly basis, with monthly or quarterly bonuses based on other measures
N/A – Payment cycles generally align to market practice
Quota Setting
Targets are set on a quarterly basis based on budgeted tour flow, with monthly adjustments as needed
Potential candidates express that HGV targets are higher than competitors
Unable to fully assess quota setting with the data provided at this time
SPIFFs
HGV has formal SPIFF policy guardrails, including approval and payroll processing requirements and are used as supplemental short-term incentives (site-specific)
Continue to use SPIFFs selectively for targeted short-term behavior change,
Strengthen governance and tracking to ensure SPIFF usage remains controlled and aligned to business priorities across all sites
Marketing | Maturity assessment
Telemarketing
Marketing
Sales
Limited Issues / Minor Opportunities for Improvement
Moderate Issues / Clear Opportunity for Improvement
Significant Issues Discovered / Action Required to Address Gaps
Insufficient Data Available
Compensation Design & Market Assessment
